feed.title: HealthRankings — Health News
feed.subtitle: Latest US health headlines. Full list: https://www.healthrankings.co/healthrankings-news.html

entry.title: Cyclosporiasis outbreak in the US, by the numbers
entry.summary: Some US states are facing a large and growing outbreak of diarrhea caused by cyclospora, a microscopic parasite that can contaminate fresh food or water.

entry.title: GLP-1 drugs linked to 17% drop in worker sick leave, new economic study finds
entry.summary: Here's how weight-loss drugs like Ozempic and Wegovy could reduce worker illness and save companies money.

entry.title: A mom felt out of breath while talking. It turns out she had stage 4 lung cancer, despite never smoking.
entry.summary: A busy mom of three developed a persistent cough and had trouble breathing, despite never smoking. She was diagnosed with a rare form of lung cancer.

entry.title: Middle-aged TV watchers hit with urgent warning as California study unveils ominous brain risk
entry.summary: This is your brain on reruns.

entry.title: Delaware sees first Cyclosporiasis case in ongoing multistate outbreak
entry.summary: The Delaware Department of Health and Social Services has confirmed the state's first case of Cyclosporiasis as health officials continue monitoring a multistate outbreak that has sickened people across the

entry.title: New study links artificial sweeteners to faster memory decline
entry.summary: Researchers found higher consumption of several common sweeteners was associated with faster cognitive decline over time.

entry.title: Here’s what you need to know when prepping meals for older relatives
entry.summary: These simple steps lead to healthier meals.

entry.title: What exactly is ‘explosive diarrhea’?
entry.summary: In the case of the ongoing cyclospora outbreak, the “hallmark” symptom appears to be the more explosive type of diarrhea.

entry.title: A Mother Got So Happy at Her Daughter’s Wedding That She Ended Up Hospitalized
entry.summary: A recent case report details how the women developed a rare condition nicknamed "happy heart syndrome."

entry.title: Coffee lovers can take heart: Up to 5 cups a day appears safe for most adults, experts say
entry.summary: The American Heart Association says up to 400 milligrams of caffeine daily may lower risk of heart disease, stroke and Type 2 diabetes — with a key caveat.

entry.title: Her date sent her a voice memo. It started a viral debate about sex
entry.summary: A viral voice memo opened up a debate on modern dating, sex and consent. Relationship and sex therapists explain why it has the internet so triggered

entry.title: Utah woman speaks out after losing husband to post-sepsis syndrome
entry.summary: Stefani Ellison, of Cedar City, lost her husband Stephen Ellison one year ago to suicide, due largely to post-sepsis syndrome. She hopes sharing their story will help others.

entry.title: What Happens to Your Blood Pressure When You Take Vitamin D
entry.summary: Learn how vitamin D supplements may affect your blood pressure, who may benefit most, and how much to take safely—especially if you’re low in vitamin D.

entry.title: Cardiologists Wish You’d Stop Doing These 3 Things To Reduce Stroke Risk
entry.summary: Two cardiologists explain the lifestyle habits that may increase your stroke risk, plus what else you can do to lower your likelihood of the disease.

entry.title: Get Winded Climbing Stairs? These 5 Moves Will Make It Easier
entry.summary: In TODAY.com’s Expert Tip of the Day, a trainer shares her favorite functional exercises to boost lower body strength and make climbing the stairs feel easier.

entry.title: Experimental drug shows 'phenomenal' results fighting California's Valley fever
entry.summary: In a new study, over 70% of people with severe Valley fever infection improved after going on the medication.

entry.title: MAHA voters feel betrayed — Republicans proceed at their own risk
entry.summary: The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention may have handed Republican leaders a political grenade with the pin pulled. On June 1, the agency awarded Pfizer contracts worth roughly $1.24 billion for COVID-19 vaccines for the remainder of fiscal 2026 and fi…

entry.title: Just 5% of Americans eat enough fiber — and I'm one of them, thanks to these tasty high-fiber snacks
entry.summary: It can be challenging to meet your daily fiber goals, which is why I always keep these high-fiber snacks stocked in my pantry.

entry.title: Wealthy US neighbourhoods paid to spray mosquitoes: By 2023, the insects had evolved resistance across 84
entry.summary: US News: In many parts of the eastern United States, getting rid of mosquitoes has become just another household service. Homeowners hire private companies to .

entry.title: 6 dementia symptoms that can show up years before a diagnosis
entry.summary: Changes to sleep and walking can begin long before cognitive issues.

entry.title: His doctor’s prescription for his unbearable pain seemed absurd. It worked.
entry.summary: Gregory Maassen was nearly bedridden by a burning sensation that was later diagnosed as a rare form of neuropathy. He has found relief riding his e-bike across the country.

entry.title: Covid-19 waking up, Cyclospora marches on, and measles is close to hitting a milestone
entry.summary: The Dose (July 21)

entry.title: The #1 Breakfast for Gut Health, Recommended by Gut Experts
entry.summary: Over 60 million Americans have a digestive disease. Learn the best gut health-boosting nutrients to include in your breakfast, according to experts.

entry.title: Exclusive: Medical student in Nepal behind busy research factory
entry.summary: A second-year medical student in Nepal is at the center of an international research network churning out everything from systematic reviews and meta-analyses to case reports and database studies, …

entry.title: New Hampshire restaurants take precautions as cyclospora outbreak continues
entry.summary: As health officials continue to track a cyclospora outbreak in other parts of the country, New Hampshire officials say there are no known connections to cases in the Granite State.

entry.title: Can You ‘Catch’ Dementia From Your Spouse?
entry.summary: Study of nearly 1 million married people found that when one spouse develops dementia, the other's risk climbs about 70 percent.

entry.title: IU Health ends life-saving mobile stroke unit service
entry.summary: INDIANAPOLIS — IU Health is discontinuing a unique program after it helped save numerous lives. Eight years ago, IU rolled out the first and only mobile stroke unit (MSU) in Indiana. The number of MSUs in the state is now returning to zero. The specialized am���

entry.title: New antibody drug stops aggressive prostate cancer from spreading
entry.summary: Scientists have developed a fully human antibody that halted the growth and spread of aggressive prostate cancer in preclinical research. Although more testing is needed, the drug’s unusual mechanism could open the door to a safer new treatment for advanced c…

entry.title: We Asked 3 Oncologists What They Eat When They Want Something Sweet and They All Said the Same Thing
entry.summary: It’s scientifically proven to lower cancer risk.

entry.title: A six-year-old entered an iron lung after polio. More than 70 years later, he had become the world’s long
entry.summary: Rest of World News: For many people, the iron lung belongs to history books, a symbol of the years when polio swept through communities before vaccines transformed public.

entry.title: Fourth person dies from Legionnaires’ disease outbreak in Manhattan
entry.summary: MANHATTAN, N.Y. (PIX11) — A fourth person has died in connection with Legionnaires’ disease in Manhattan, according to the city’s health department. The first reported death in relation to the Legionnaires cluster on the Upper East Side was reported on Friday…

entry.title: Women with common health condition face higher risk ofheart disease
entry.summary: Polyendocrine metabolic ovarian syndrome, which affects one in eight women in the UK, increases the chances of developing cardiovascular disease fourfold, a study finds

entry.title: Brain tumor found during preventive scan puts full-body MRIs back in the spotlight
entry.summary: Full-body scans are making headlines for potentially catching diseases early, but some doctors urge caution. Here's what you need to know about the pricey screenings.

entry.title: DRC's neighbors boost preparedness as Ebola cases surge
entry.summary: Uganda, Rwanda and Zambia are strengthening border screening, disease surveillance and emergency response measures. The growing Ebola outbreak in the DRC is raising concerns about cross-border spread.

entry.title: Douglas County officials: 'Rapid' Cyclospora surge reported, Taco Bell emerges as top exposure
entry.summary: Officials said Douglas County epidemiologists have identified eating at Taco Bell as the most common exposure for the cases reported in the area.

entry.title: Delaware confirms first measles case in more than a decade
entry.summary: According to the Division of Public Health, a confirmed case of measles involves an unvaccinated man in Kent County.

entry.title: BREAKING: Delaware confirms first case of measles in over a decade
entry.summary: The Delaware Division of Public Health has announced the first confirmed case of measles in the state in over ten years.

entry.title: Roadside runner may be why leprosy cases are rising in Central Florida
entry.summary: Four counties have 52% of Florida's new leprosy cases. They're all located along I-4, home to the nine-banded armadillo. It's no coincidence.

entry.title: 6 fun activities that can slow aging, according to science — it’s good news for Broadway fans
entry.summary: The strongest effects, which researchers compared to those gained from frequent exercise, were seen in those who regularly participated.

entry.title: Even Mild Sleep Deprivation May Lead to Weight Gain
entry.summary: A recent study suggests that not getting enough sleep increases the risk of weight gain, and thus of conditions like heart disease and type 2 diabetes.

entry.title: Up to 5 cups of coffee per day may potentially lower heart disease risk, doctors say
entry.summary: Drinking up to five cups of coffee a day is safe for most adults, and regular consumption may be linked to a lower risk of stroke, diabetes and heart failure.

entry.title: 19 Human Body Facts That Genuinely Blew Med Students' Minds In School
entry.summary: "Sperm can get out of the uterus through the fallopian tube into the pelvic cavity. Everyone did a collective WHAT in lecture that day."

entry.title: "Everyone Did A Collective WHAT In Lecture": Med Students Are Sharing Wild Body Facts
entry.summary: "Sperm can get out of the uterus through the fallopian tube into the pelvic cavity. Everyone did a collective WHAT in lecture that day."

entry.title: 'If only people knew what a tick could do'
entry.summary: Mammalian meat allergy is a growing problem along Australia's east coast - and it's caused by tick bites.

entry.title: Drinking 5 cups of coffee a day is safe and could reduce heart risk: Report
entry.summary: Higher caffeine intake, including levels found in energy drinks, was associated with cardiovascular harm.

entry.title: The hidden cost of the night shift and how to sleep it off
entry.summary: More than three million people in the UK work night shifts, which can greatly impact their health. What can be done to help them?

entry.title: Seattle summers can cause ‘sunshine guilt.’ Here’s how to beat it
entry.summary: Seattleites are faced with a seemingly unique phenomenon: having too much to do during the summer months.

entry.title: Plasma proteome profiling identified biomarkers for the differential diagnosis and molecular staging of neurodegenerative dementias
entry.summary: A large multicenter proteomics study identified blood biomarkers that distinguish Alzheimer’s disease, dementia with Lewy bodies and frontotemporal dementia, supporting biomarker-based differential diagnosis of neurodegenerative diseases.

entry.title: Smuggling charges against NIH virologists trigger political uproar
entry.summary: Other researchers say that non-infectious viral samples in luggage posed no risk, but might have violated import rules.

entry.title: Ebola death toll rises to 930, as outbreak spreads at record pace
entry.summary: Africa Centres for Disease Control and Prevention and partners plan trial of two existing jabs
